Title: Root-associated Streptomyces produce galbonolides to modulate plant immunity and promote rhizosphere colonization

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PAD3 abolishes AgN23-induced camalexin accumulation and reduces AgN23 abundance in the rhizosphere, indicating camalexin production promotes bacterial colonization
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pad3-1 mutant analysis; LC-HRMS metabolite profiling; rhizosphere colonization assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AgN23 inoculation; culture medium extract treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAD3 is required for AgN23-induced camalexin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis roots. 2. pad3-1 mutant lacks camalexin induction after AgN23 inoculation. 3. pad3-1 plants show reduced AgN23 rhizosphere colonization relative to wild type.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to shape rhizosphere microbiota via camalexin-mediated ecological filtering
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced camalexin-related defense may alter microbiota composition or growth-defense balance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states PAD3 encodes a CYP450 converting cysteine-indole-3-acetonitrile to camalexin; also used as a defense marker gene in qPCR
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: root LC-HRMS metabolomics; associated prior RNA-seq dataset GSE119986
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Arabidopsis phytoalexin implicated in antifungal defense and rhizosphere microbiota regulation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AgN23 culture extract inhibits AtIPCS2 activity in a galbonolide-dependent manner, linking bacterial galbonolides to host sphingolipid perturbation and downstream defense/camalexin induction
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay using microsomal fractions from yeast expressing AtIPCS2; comparison with ΔgbnB mutant extracts
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: treatment with AgN23 culture medium extracts
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtIPCS2 enzymatic activity is strongly inhibited by AgN23 wild-type extract. 2. ΔgbnB mutant extracts fail to inhibit AtIPCS2. 3. IPCS inhibition is the proposed proximal trigger for defense activation and camalexin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential host target for understanding microbial modulation of sphingolipid-mediated immunity; not directly proposed for breeding in paper
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: IPCS inhibition is associated with defense activation and mild growth inhibition/cell death-like responses
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as the host target of galbonolides rather than through plant genetics in this paper
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: camalexin functions in phytoalexin defense and may promote AgN23 rhizosphere establishment indirectly

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: galbonolides (galbonolides A, B, E, and G)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Required structural PKS step for biosynthesis of galbonolide macrolides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption of gbnB abolishes galbonolide production, eliminates inhibition of Arabidopsis IPCS2, reduces induction of plant defense responses including camalexin biosynthesis, and decreases rhizosphere colonization by AgN23
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; LC-HRMS metabolite profiling; Arabidopsis IPCS2 enzyme inhibition assay; plant defense assays; rhizosphere colonization assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: root/rhizosphere colonization; culture medium extract treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gbnB encodes the structural PKS required for biosynthesis of all detected galbonolides in AgN23. 2. ΔgbnB mutants lost galbonolide peaks in LC-HRMS and lost inhibitory activity toward Arabidopsis IPCS2. 3. Loss of gbnB reduced HR-like responses, defense gene induction, camalexin accumulation, and rhizosphere colonization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential microbial engineering target to modulate beneficial rhizosphere colonization through elicitation of plant defense metabolites; caution because associated with mild growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of gbnB reduces bacterial rhizosphere fitness and antifungal activity; wild-type galbonolide production is associated with slight plant growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper schematic shows galbonolide BGC genes gbnA-E and methoxymalonyl-CoA genes gbnH-K, but only gbnB was directly manipulated and functionally validated as a core gene
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-HRMS metabolomics; genome assembly NZ_CP007153.1; associated prior RNA-seq dataset GSE119986
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for galbonolide production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Microbial metabolite that inhibits plant/fungal IPCS, triggers plant immune responses, and promotes Streptomyces rhizosphere colonization; also contributes to antifungal activity
